description: Add world-class resident wellbeing features: (1) Mood & Wellbeing Tracker - a dedicated mood/wellbeing tracking system that lets staff quickly log daily mood, pain level, sleep quality, social engagement, and appetite for each resident with trend visualization. (2) Personal Preferences & Life Story board - a rich profile section where staff can record life history, hobbies, favorite music/TV, conversation topics, comfort items, and daily routine preferences so every staff member can provide truly personalized care. (3) Social Isolation Alert system - automatically flags residents who haven't had visitors, activity participation, or meaningful social notes in configurable thresholds. (4) Sensory Garden / Environment widget showing personalized environment recommendations (lighting, music, temperature) per resident based on their conditions and preferences.
steps: Step 1 - Database schema: Create backend/migrations/003_wellbeing.sql with: (a) 'wellbeing_logs' table (id UUID, care_home_id UUID FK, resident_id UUID FK, logged_by UUID FK users, log_date DATE, mood VARCHAR CHECK 'very_happy','happy','neutral','low','very_low', pain_level INT 0-10, sleep_quality VARCHAR CHECK 'excellent','good','fair','poor','very_poor', social_engagement VARCHAR CHECK 'high','moderate','low','isolated', appetite VARCHAR CHECK 'excellent','good','fair','poor','refused', energy_level VARCHAR CHECK 'high','moderate','low','very_low', notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). (b) 'resident_life_story' table (id UUID, resident_id UUID FK UNIQUE, occupation VARCHAR, hometown VARCHAR, spouse_info TEXT, children_info TEXT, pets TEXT, hobbies TEXT[], favorite_music TEXT[], favorite_tv TEXT[], favorite_foods TEXT[], conversation_topics TEXT[], comfort_items TEXT[], daily_routine_preferences TEXT, religious_preferences TEXT, dislikes TEXT[], important_dates JSONB, life_achievements TEXT, war_service TEXT, personality_traits TEXT[], communication_style TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by UUID FK users). (c) 'social_isolation_alerts' table (id UUID, care_home_id UUID FK, resident_id UUID FK, alert_type VARCHAR CHECK 'no_visitors','no_activities','no_social_notes','low_engagement', days_since_last INT, severity VARCHAR CHECK 'mild','moderate','severe', status VARCHAR CHECK 'active','acknowledged','resolved', acknowledged_by UUID FK users, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). (d) 'environment_preferences' table (id UUID, resident_id UUID FK UNIQUE, preferred_lighting VARCHAR, preferred_temperature VARCHAR, preferred_music_volume VARCHAR, calming_sounds TEXT[], aromatherapy TEXT[], room_decorations TEXT, photo_display_preference TEXT, tv_volume VARCHAR, noise_sensitivity VARCHAR CHECK 'low','moderate','high', notes TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).

Step 2 - Backend controller: Create backend/src/controllers/wellbeing.controller.ts with: logWellbeing (POST /wellbeing/log), getResidentWellbeing (GET /wellbeing/:residentId - last 30 days of logs with trend data), getWellbeingOverview (GET /wellbeing/overview - all residents summary for today), getResidentLifeStory (GET /residents/:id/life-story), updateResidentLifeStory (PUT /residents/:id/life-story), getSocialIsolationAlerts (GET /wellbeing/isolation-alerts), acknowledgeSocialAlert (PATCH /wellbeing/isolation-alerts/:id), generateIsolationAlerts (POST /wellbeing/generate-isolation-alerts - scans all residents and creates alerts based on configurable thresholds: 7 days no visitors = mild, 14 days = moderate, 21 days = severe; 5 days no activities = mild, 10 days = moderate), getEnvironmentPreferences (GET /residents/:id/environment), updateEnvironmentPreferences (PUT /residents/:id/environment).

Step 3 - Backend routes: Add all wellbeing routes to backend/src/routes/index.ts. Wellbeing logging is isStaff, life story is isStaff for read and isClinical/isManager for write, isolation alerts is isManager.

Step 4 - Seed data: Update backend/src/utils/seed.ts to seed: (a) 30 days of wellbeing logs for all 24 residents with realistic variation. (b) Life stories for at least 12 residents with rich biographical details. (c) Environment preferences for 8 residents. (d) A few active social isolation alerts for residents who haven't had activities or visitors recently.
Step 5 - Frontend types: Add WellbeingLog, ResidentLifeStory, SocialIsolationAlert, EnvironmentPreferences types to frontend/src/types/index.ts.

Step 6 - Frontend hooks and API: Add wellbeingApi to frontend/src/services/api.ts and add hooks to frontend/src/hooks/index.ts (useLogWellbeing, useResidentWellbeing, useWellbeingOverview, useResidentLifeStory, useUpdateLifeStory, useSocialIsolationAlerts, useAcknowledgeAlert, useEnvironmentPreferences, useUpdateEnvironmentPreferences).

Step 7 - Wellbeing Overview page: Create frontend/src/pages/WellbeingHub.tsx - a dedicated wellbeing hub page with: (a) Quick-log grid showing all residents with one-tap mood logging (emoji-based, fast). (b) Trend charts showing mood/pain/sleep patterns over 7/14/30 days for selected resident. (c) Social isolation alerts panel with acknowledge/resolve actions. (d) 'Residents Needing Attention' priority list based on declining wellbeing scores. (e) Daily wellbeing summary stats (average mood, pain, participation). Use inline styles matching existing patterns.

Step 8 - Life Story component: Create frontend/src/components/LifeStoryBoard.tsx - a beautiful life story display component used in Resident Detail that shows the resident's life history in an engaging card layout with sections for occupation, family, hobbies, music, conversation starters, comfort items, and important dates. Include an edit mode for authorized staff.

Step 9 - Environment Preferences component: Create frontend/src/components/EnvironmentCard.tsx - a compact card showing personalized environment recommendations for a resident (lighting, music, temperature, sensory needs) used in the Resident Detail page.

Step 10 - ResidentDetail integration: Add 'Life Story' and 'Wellbeing' tabs to the ResidentDetail page. Life Story tab shows the LifeStoryBoard component. Wellbeing tab shows mood/pain/sleep trends and environment preferences.

Step 11 - App routing and navigation: Add /wellbeing route to App.tsx, import WellbeingHub, and add 'Wellbeing Hub' to NAV_ALL (under Care section, icon: '💚', roles: all staff). Also add a wellbeing quick-log widget to the CarerDashboard showing residents with low mood today.
acceptance_criteria: Backend compiles without errors (npm run build in backend/)
Frontend compiles without errors (npm run build in frontend/)
Wellbeing logging allows staff to quickly record mood/pain/sleep/engagement for residents
Life story profiles store and display rich biographical information
Social isolation alerts flag residents who haven't had visitors or activities
Environment preferences store personalized sensory/comfort settings
Wellbeing Hub page shows overview of all residents' wellbeing status
Resident Detail has Life Story and Wellbeing tabs
Navigation includes Wellbeing Hub for all staff roles
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/backend && npm run build' - must succeed
Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/frontend && npm run build' - must succeed
findings: Both frontend and backend build successfully (exit 0). Pre-existing TS errors remain in CareNotes, Emar, Incidents, Training (same as FEAT-001). No new errors from wellbeing feature. All 11 steps implemented: migration, controller, routes, seed data, types, API/hooks, WellbeingHub page, LifeStoryBoard, EnvironmentCard, ResidentDetail tabs (Life Story + Wellbeing), App.tsx routing, and CarerDashboard wellbeing widget.
